pytorch.reason: integrate β(g) = -b*g² via Euler steps along log-scale; autograd confirms dg/d(log μ) matches β(g) numerically; verify asymptotic freedom and IR fixed points

pyg.reason: not used — RG flow bridge is a scalar ODE; no graph message-passing required

z3.reason: UNSAT: g(μ) = 0 AND g(μ₀) > 0 with b > 0 at finite scale — coupling zero is only reached asymptotically; z3 encodes this impossibility structurally

cvc5.reason: not used — z3 covers the proof layer for this sim

sympy.reason: exact one-loop solution g(μ) = g₀/(1 + b*g₀*log(μ/μ₀)); verify UV limit, IR limit, fixed-point stability β'(g*)=0 symbolically

clifford.reason: represent RG group element as Cl(1,0) rotor exp(t*e1) where t=log(μ/μ₀); composition law for scale transformations maps to rotor multiplication; confirms group structure of RG flow

geomstats.reason: not used — RG flow bridge is a 1D ODE; Riemannian geometry tools not required at this level

e3nn.reason: not used — RG flow bridge is scalar; equivariant networks not required

rustworkx.reason: RG fixed point graph: UV fixed points, IR fixed points, and marginal operators as nodes; flow direction edges encode which fixed points attract/repel at each scale

xgi.reason: not used — RG flow bridge is scalar ODE; no hypergraph topology required

toponetx.reason: not used — RG flow bridge is scalar; no cell complex required

gudhi.reason: not used — RG flow bridge is analytic; no persistent homology required

P1_pytorch_asymptotic_freedom_g_decrease.description: Pytorch: β(g)=-b*g² (b>0): coupling g decreases as μ increases — asymptotic freedom

P2_pytorch_ir_fixed_point_g_decreases_in.description: Pytorch: β(g)=+b*g² (b>0): g decreases toward IR (μ→0) — IR attractive fixed point at g=0

P3_pytorch_beta_function_matches_derivat.description: Pytorch: numeric dg/d(log μ) matches β(g)=-b*g² within 0.01 at all steps

P4_sympy_exact_one_loop_solution_verifie.description: Sympy: g(t)=g₀/(1+b*g₀*t) satisfies dg/dt=-b*g² exactly — one-loop RG solution verified

P5_sympy_uv_limit_g_to_zero.description: Sympy: lim(t→∞) g₀/(1+b*g₀*t) = 0 — asymptotic freedom confirmed: g→0 at UV

P6_sympy_fixed_point_marginal.description: Sympy: β'(g*)=β'(0)=0 — fixed point g*=0 is marginal (neither attractive nor repulsive at linear order)

P7_clifford_rg_group_composition_law.description: Clifford Cl(1,0): scale transformation rotors compose as R(t1)*R(t2)=R(t1+t2) — RG group law verified

P8_rustworkx_rg_fixed_point_graph.description: Rustworkx: RG graph has UV fixed point (g*=0) with incoming flow edges — coupling flows toward g=0 at high energy

P9_pytorch_numeric_matches_exact.description: Pytorch Euler integration matches exact sympy solution to < 0.005 for one-loop RG

N1_z3_coupling_cannot_reach_zero_at_fini.description: Z3 UNSAT: g(μ)=0 with g₀>0, b>0, finite log scale — one-loop coupling never reaches zero at finite energy

N2_sympy_g_strictly_positive_for_all_fin.description: Sympy: 1+b*g₀*t > 0 for all positive b,g₀,t — g(μ) is strictly positive at all finite scales

N3_pytorch_g_never_negative_under_rg_flo.description: Negative: g remains > 0 throughout RG flow from μ₀ to large μ — coupling is positive definite

N4_sympy_beta_nonzero_for_positive_g.description: Negative: β(g)=-b*g²=0 has no solution for g>0 — coupling is always running; fixed point g*=0 is not in the positive-g domain

N5_clifford_scale_transform_invertible.description: Negative: Clifford R(t)*R(-t)=1 — scale transformations are invertible; RG flow can be run both UV and IR

N6_rustworkx_uv_ir_fixed_points_disconne.description: Negative: UV fixed point (g=0) has no path to IR fixed point — they are structurally distinct endpoints

B1_pytorch_slow_running_near_uv_fixed_po.description: Boundary: near UV fixed point (small g), β(g) is much smaller than at g=O(1) — slow running

B2_sympy_landau_pole_exists_for_phi4.description: Boundary: scalar φ⁴ has Landau pole at t* = 1/(b*g₀) — coupling diverges at finite UV scale

B3_pytorch_b_0.1_final_g.description: Boundary: b=0.1, larger b → faster decrease of g under RG flow

B3_pytorch_b_1.0_final_g.description: Boundary: b=1.0, larger b → faster decrease of g under RG flow

B3_pytorch_b_5.0_final_g.description: Boundary: b=5.0, larger b → faster decrease of g under RG flow

B4_z3_decreasing_g_is_consistent.description: Boundary z3 SAT: g_final < g₀ with positive b,g₀,t is fully consistent — asymptotic freedom is self-consistent

B5_clifford_identity_scale_is_unit_rotor.description: Boundary: R(t=0) = 1 in Cl(1,0) — zero scale shift is the identity transformation

B6_rustworkx_rg_scale_hierarchy.description: Boundary: RG flow DAG topological order has UV before IR — scale hierarchy is consistent
